title: rubus horridicaulis
intro: Rubus horridicaulis, also known as the thorny bramble, is a species of flowering plant in the rose family, characterized by its prickly stems and edible fruits. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antibacterial effects. Consuming Rubus horridicaulis has been linked to several health benefits, including reducing the risk of heart disease, improving digestion, and supporting immune function. Rubus horridicaulis can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and is often used to make jams, jellies, and other edible products.
